petitioner_background: The petitioner is a self-represented individual (referred to as 'she') seeking classification as an employment-based immigrant under section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act as an alien of extraordinary ability. No specific field of work, nationality, or career details are provided in the decision. The petition was filed with the Vermont Service Center.
outcome_reasoning: The appeal was summarily dismissed because the petitioner failed to specifically address the reasons stated for denial and provided no additional evidence or arguments addressing the regulatory criteria at 8 C.F.R. § 204.5(h)(3). The petitioner's appellate submission consisted only of a brief statement expressing confidence in her skills and requesting reconsideration, without identifying any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact. The petitioner explicitly indicated she was not submitting a separate brief or evidence. Under 8 C.F.R. § 103.3(a)(1)(v), an appeal must be summarily dismissed when the party fails to identify specifically any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact.
legal_citations: Section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act
fraud_or_procedural_issues: Procedural dismissal: The petitioner failed to identify specifically any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact in the director's denial, and did not submit a brief or additional evidence with the appeal, triggering summary dismissal under 8 C.F.R. § 103.3(a)(1)(v).
